signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hike Bets
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5641 as RBNZ hike bets collapse following a break of critical support. USD outperformance (1W avg +0.945%) vs NZD underperformance (1W avg -1.471%) creates a 2.416% G10 spread. Despite RBNZ OIS bias signaling a hike, retail is 96% long against a COT LF net short of -22,454 (long% 18.4%), signaling extreme institutional vs retail divergence. The collapse in hike pricing suggests a long-unwind regime toward 0.5580.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Vol + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.75 holding above the 160.00 intervention threshold. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 1.0% provides a +275bp carry advantage for USD, supported by a positive USD CESI (+28). However, 25d RR at -1.52 (strong put skew) and a VRP of +4.9% (IV 7.2% vs HV30 2.3%) indicate the options market is aggressively paying for downside protection against BoJ intervention risk. Carry remains the dominant driver while VIX remains at 19.2 (normal volatility).

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4233 exhibits a significant correlation break vs WTI Oil (z = -2.01σ), with the pair rallying despite oil holding near four-month lows. Retail sentiment is 96% short (extreme), diverging from a COT LF net short of -67,668 (long% 23.4%). The institutional and retail alignment in shorting the CAD suggests a structural move higher in USD/CAD, supported by a +150bp Fed-BoC carry advantage.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Vol Complacency + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6892 faces headwinds as the AUD CESI (-8) confirms data underperformance. Despite a -60bp carry deficit for USD, AUD/USD IV at 3.1% sits 5.6% below HV30 (8.7%), signaling unusual vol complacency. With COT LF net long at +38,548 (70.2% long), the pair is vulnerable to a positioning squeeze if realized volatility mean-reverts higher amid broader G10 AUD weakness.
